notes: Works perfectly fine in 4k with DXVK. Mouse is quite fast and the launch menu somehow broken without nice fonts or any background, but you can click on play at least. This game doesn't require any workaround for Vulkan apps on Wayland and it runs just fine. The only issue was that the game exit crashed my machine.
title: Zombie Army Trilogy

notes: I did notice a bit of stuttering when playing it for the first time, but it seems to have disappeared completely after playing for a while. The launcher looks ugly, but it works.
title: Zombie Army Trilogy

notes: Like others have said; D3D11 to get it working, and ignore the visual glitches on the launcher. Did have the white "texturing" issue, which unfortunately is bad enough to make the game not playable. Textures themselves seem fine, however volumetric shapes (triangles, spheres, rectangles) around light sources were creating white areas on the screen, and any models inside them would be shaded over with white.
title: Zombie Army Trilogy

notes: Did not have to tweak anything for it to run perfectly on my system.
title: Zombie Army Trilogy

notes: Did not need additional launch options and even with the launcher looking weird, the game runs great with the latest AMD Mesa drivers. Some people mentioned texture issues, but did not get them. I even played a few multiplayer matches with another guy on a Windows machine!

As I said earlier I cover the requirements but I still get 25 FPS tops with medium to low settings. Just now I found a rather large (120+ MB) steam log file, which will hopefully help you resolve this game's problems. I 7-zipped it with LZMA2 and here it is:

title: Zombie Army Trilogy

notes:
The launcher simply displayed links with no graphics, but I there were no issues starting the game. Menus all worked fine, however settings for audio level did not affect load in/boot volume and returned to settings once at the menus(Unsure if this is expected). Game-play was smooth in my build and I experienced no visual or audio abnormalities.  Anytime a game was entered and I returned to the menus,  mouse control was lost, rendering the game unplayable. I was however able to plug in an Amazon Basics xbox controller (Should function similarly to a real xbox controller) and was able to fully control the game with that.

In summary: 
Not playable with Keyboard and mouse, fully functional with a controller.
title: Zombie Army Trilogy

notes: Tested single and multiplayer, gameplay was very smooth on my setup, but there are 3 issues: 1. The initial launcher looks strange (just text on white background), but that doesn't affect functionality. 2. If the graphics run in flipping/g-sync mode, the mouse disappears after the first return to the menus from gameplay (e.g. after dying), rendering the game unplayable. With blitting mode that does not happen. 3. After prolonged continuous gameplay (1h+) the game begins to stutter although the displayed fps is still very high (>100fps). Restarting the game brings it back to normal.
title: Zombie Army Trilogy

notes: Only tested the first map, runs at around 100 fps on ultra, 1440p. Slight fps dip when turning fast, probably due to the 1000Hz mouse bug.
